CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, amount FLOAT, sale_date DATE);
SELECT id, amount, lag(amount, 3, 0) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date) AS lag_amount FROM sales;
CREATE TABLE employees (emp_id INTEGER, emp_name VARCHAR, dept_id INTEGER);
SELECT e.emp_id, e.emp_name, lag(s.amount, 3, 0) OVER (PARTITION BY e.dept_id ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS lag_amount FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.emp_id = s.id;
CREATE TABLE products (product_id INTEGER, product_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO products VALUES (1, 'Product A'), (2, 'Product B'), (3, 'Product C');
SELECT p.product_name, lag(s.amount, 3, 0) OVER (ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS lag_amount FROM products p LEFT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.id;
SELECT id, sale_date, amount, lag(amount, 2) OVER (ORDER BY sale_date) AS lag_2_amount FROM sales WHERE amount IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INTEGER, customer_id INTEGER, total FLOAT);
SELECT o.customer_id, o.total, lag(o.total, 2, 0) OVER (PARTITION BY o.customer_id ORDER BY o.order_id) AS lag_2_amount FROM orders o;

SELECT s.id, s.sale_date, lag(s.amount, 1, 0) OVER (ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS prev_sale, lag(s.amount, 2, 0) OVER (ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS prev_sale2, lag(s.amount, 3, 0) OVER (IGNORE NULLS ORDER BY s.sale_date) AS prev_sale3 FROM sales s;
